params.policyParameterNames: The parameter names for the policy.
params.target: The address of the target contract for which the policy apply.

params.policyParameterNames: The parameter names for the policy.
params.position: The position to add the policy at.
params.target: The address of the target contract for which the policy apply.

params.payload: The payload to run the policies on.

params.target: The address of the target contract for which the policies are configured.

returns._0: The policies for the selector and target.
The current configuration version for the policy.
The custom policy parameter mapper for the policy, address(0) if the policy uses the default mapper.

params.target: The address of the target contract for which the policy was configured.

params.payload: The payload to run the policies on.

params.defaultAllow: Indicates whether to allow or reject a transaction if no policy explicitly returns an Allow or a Reject. True to allow, false to reject.

params.configData: The calldata for the configuration function.
params.configSelector: The selector of the configuration function.
params.policy: The address of the policy to configure.

params.mapper: The mapper address, address(0) to use the default mapper.

params.defaultAllow: Indicates whether to allow or reject a transaction if no policy explicitly returns an Allow or a Reject. True to allow, false to reject.
params.target: The address of the target contract.

returns._0: A string representing the type and version of the policy engine.

addPolicy(address,bytes4,address,bytes32.notice: Adds a policy to the policy engine. - Policy MUST be added to the end of the current policy list.

addPolicyAt(address,bytes4,address,bytes.notice: Adds a policy to the policy engine at a specific position.

attach().notice: Attaches the calling contract to the policy engine.

check((bytes4,address,bytes,bytes)).notice: Runs the policies for a given payload for offchain pre-validation. MUST revert on policy rejection/failure.

detach().notice: Detaches the calling contract from the policy engine.

getExtractor(bytes4).notice: Gets the extractor for a given selector.

getPolicies(address,bytes4).notice: Gets the policies for a given selector and target. - MUST return the policies in the order they will execute. - MUST return an empty array if no policies are found.

getPolicyConfigVersion(address).notice: Gets the current configuration version for a policy.

getPolicyMapper(address).notice: Gets the policy parameter mapper for a given policy.

removePolicy(address,bytes4,address).notice: Removes a policy from the policy engine.

run((bytes4,address,bytes,bytes)).notice: Runs the policies for a given operation payload. - MUST revert on policy rejection/failure. - MUST revert if the target contract that invoked the method is not allowed. Target contract address is   obtained from the msg.sender global variable. - MUST execute policies in the order they were added or that were specified using `addPolicyAt`.

setDefaultPolicyAllow(bool).notice: Sets whether to allow or reject the transaction if no policy explicitly returns an Allow or a Reject.

setExtractor(bytes4,address).notice: Assigns an extractor to the specified selector, enabling policies to utilize it for parameter extraction.

setExtractors(bytes4[],address).notice: Assigns an extractor to the specified selectors, enabling policies to utilize it for parameter extraction.

setPolicyConfiguration(address,uint256,b.notice: Sets the configuration for a policy.

setPolicyMapper(address,address).notice: Sets the custom policy parameter mapper for a policy.

setTargetDefaultPolicyAllow(address,bool.notice: Sets whether to allow or reject the transaction if no policy explicitly returns an Allow or a Reject for a specific target.

typeAndVersion().notice: Returns the type and version of the policy engine.
